codeMarkPlus.defaultLanguage.description: Default language to be used for comments.

codeMarkPlus.isCommentMessageWrapped.description: Determine whether to wrap the comment message with additional formatting or markers.

codeMarkPlus.commentDelimiter.description: Delimiter to separate different elements inside the comment message (e.g., file name, function).

codeMarkPlus.commentMessagePrefix.description: Prefix added at the beginning of the comment message.

codeMarkPlus.commentMessageSuffix.description: Suffix added at the end of the comment message.

codeMarkPlus.addEmptyLineBeforeComment.description: Insert an empty line before the comment message for improved readability.

codeMarkPlus.addEmptyLineAfterComment.description: Insert an empty line after the comment message for improved readability.

codeMarkPlus.literalOpen.description: The opening character used to delimit string literals (e.g., double quotes, single quotes, backticks).

codeMarkPlus.literalClose.description: The closing character used to delimit string literals (e.g., double quotes, single quotes, backticks).

codeMarkPlus.useCurrentPosition.description: Use the current cursor position to insert the comment.

codeMarkPlus.author.description: The author's name for the comment.

codeMarkPlus.highlightActive.description: Enable dynamic highlighting while the editor is active.

keyword.description: The keyword to highlight. Used when matchMode is 'word' or 'substring'. By default, matches require a trailing colon after the keyword (e.g., 'TODO:'). If the keyword itself already includes a colon, it is respected. Use 'regex' with 'pattern' to customize separators.

color.description: The background color used for the highlight (e.g., 'rgba(255,204,0,0.3)').

bold.description: The text style for bold keywords.

italic.description: The text style for italic keywords.

underline.description: The text style for underlined keywords.

matchMode.description:
Specifies the matching strategy. 
- 'word': Matches the exact keyword as a whole word (default for backward compatibility). 
- 'substring': Matches the keyword as part of a larger string. 
- 'regex': Uses the 'pattern' property for matching. 

Note: For 'word' and 'substring' with a 'keyword', a trailing colon after the keyword is required unless the keyword already contains ':'.

pattern.description: A valid JavaScript regular expression pattern to use when matchMode is 'regex'. Do not include leading/trailing slashes. Example: 'TASK\s*\(\d+\)'.

caseSensitive.description: Whether matching is case-sensitive. Defaults to true. Set to false to perform case-insensitive matching. Applies to both 'keyword' and 'pattern' modes.

wholeWord.description: When true, ensures the match does not occur inside another word. It is implicitly true for 'word' mode and can be used to constrain 'substring' mode.

languageIds.description: Array of VS Code language identifiers to which this rule applies. If empty or omitted, the rule is global. Example: ['typescript', 'javascript'].

strikethrough.description: When true, applies a strikethrough style to the highlighted text.

priority.description: A number that determines precedence when rules overlap. Higher values are applied on top of lower ones. Default is 0.

codeMarkPlus.highlightRules.description:
Defines rules for highlighting text. Each rule must specify what to find and a color.

Note: When using 'keyword' with 'word' or 'substring', the keyword must be followed by a colon in the source text (e.g., 'TODO:'). If you need other separators, use 'regex' with 'pattern'.

**Examples:**

*Legacy (whole word, case-sensitive by default):*
`{ "keyword": "TODO", "color": "rgba(255,204,0,0.3)" }` (matches 'TODO:' in text)

*Substring (case-sensitive):*
`{ "keyword": "ref", "color": "#c586c0", "matchMode": "substring", "caseSensitive": true }` (matches 'ref:' in text)

*Regex (for a specific language):*
`{ "pattern": "@custom\\w*", "color": "#6796e6", "matchMode": "regex", "languageIds": ["typescript"] }`

codeMarkPlus.specialHighlightDecoration.description: Configuration for the decoration applied to special highlight directives (e.g., 'HIGHLIGHT: next line').

language.description: The programming language for which this template applies (e.g., 'javascript', 'python', 'dart').

template.description: The comment message template. Use variables like {{{commentMessagePrefix}}}, {{{commentMessageSuffix}}}, etc. Example: "// {{{commentMessagePrefix}}} {{{fileName}}} {{{commentMessageSuffix}}}"

codeMarkPlus.customTemplates.description: Custom comment message templates for different languages. You can define a template per language using available variables (e.g., {{{commentMessagePrefix}}}, {{{commentMessageSuffix}}}, {{{fileName}}}, {{{author}}}).

codeMarkPlus.files.includedFilePatterns.description: Glob patterns of files to include when searching (e.g., ['**/*']).

codeMarkPlus.files.excludedFilePatterns.description: Glob patterns of files and folders to exclude from searches (e.g., ['**/node_modules/**','**/.git/**']).

codeMarkPlus.files.maxSearchRecursionDep.description: Maximum directory depth for recursive search (0 = unlimited).

codeMarkPlus.files.supportsHiddenFiles.description: Include dotfiles and hidden files (those starting with a dot) in search results.

codeMarkPlus.files.preserveGitignoreSett.description: Respect .gitignore rules when searching for files.

codeMarkPlus.files.showFilePathInResults.description: Show the relative folder path next to each file in results.

codeMarkPlus.performance.maxFilesToIndex.description: Maximum number of files to scan for tags per workspace to cap indexing time.

codeMarkPlus.performance.concurrencyLimi.description: Maximum number of files processed concurrently during indexing.

codeMarkPlus.performance.batchSize.description: Number of files to process per batch before yielding to keep the UI responsive.

codeMarkPlus.tags.tagProfiles.description: Map of tag profiles to customize priority, icon, and color per tag (e.g., { "TODO": { "priority": 10, "icon": "$(tag)", "color": "#ffcc00" } }).

priority.description: Priority used for sorting and resolving conflicts. Higher values indicate more important tags.

icon.description: Codicon name (e.g., $(tag)) or emoji to display for this tag in the Tag Browser.

color.description: Color for this tag (hex like #RRGGBB or rgba()).

codeMarkPlus.notes.notesFolder.description: Folder (relative to the workspace) where CodeMark+ stores notes and generated files.

codeMarkPlus.notes.createDefaultFiles.description: Create default note files (TODO and Scratchpad) inside the notes folder.

codeMarkPlus.notes.todoFileName.description: File name for the default TODO notes file.

codeMarkPlus.notes.scratchpadFileName.description: File name for the default Scratchpad notes file.

codeMarkPlus.features.quickActions.description: User-defined quick actions that run VS Code commands with optional arguments and UI metadata.

name.description: Display name of the quick action.

command.description: VS Code command ID to execute.

args.description: Optional array of arguments to pass to the command.

shortcut.description: Optional description of a keyboard shortcut for this action.

icon.description: Optional Codicon name (e.g., $(tag)) or emoji to show for the action.

codeMarkPlus.tags.tagPriorities.description: List of tag priority overrides with optional color and icon metadata.

tag.description: Tag text to match (e.g., TODO, FIXME).

priority.description: Priority used for sorting and resolving conflicts. Higher values indicate more important tags.

color.description: Optional color for this tag (hex like #RRGGBB or rgba()).

icon.description: Optional Codicon name (e.g., $(tag)) or emoji to display for this tag.
